rationale: BEHAVIORAL-EXECUTION TRUTH / THE REAL GAP (Rex D-S18-1, Cycle-16-S19 BE-O): the kernel-coach AgentContract COMMITS to runtime_emit_event_class='kernel_coach.dispatch', but that class is NEVER emitted in any sink (outputs/kernel_coach_events.jsonl is empty; no event of that class anywhere at relabel time). present-but-not-emitted -> implemented=false. This is the canonical 'committed but the behavior never actually fired' gap — exactly the spec->implementation gap Cycle 16 exists to detect. Relabel-to-TRUTH (the probe was run to confirm the class genuinely does not fire), NOT relabel-to-pass. Replaces the old nonexistent-agent fixture because under the emission-record observable a real committed-but-silent agent is the meaningful negative.
